response: Bat, the historian needs to determine how many average purchases could be made with the total number of tokens issued by the village in one year. Each token is worth 3 pence, and the village issued 240 tokens, so the total value of the tokens is 240 × 3 = 720 pence. If the average purchase cost 18 pence, then the number of purchases that could be made is 720 ÷ 18 = 40. Therefore, 40 average purchases could be made with the total number of tokens issued by the village in that year. P.P.S.: lake.
